symptoms.symptom_name: Months of progressive fatigue, lethargy and reduced exercise tolerance with exertional breathlessness/faintness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Non-specific anaemia symptoms; supportive but not localising.

symptoms.symptom_name: Symmetrical ascending distal numbness and paraesthesia (toes to knees over 3 months)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Vitamin B12 deficiency with subacute combined degeneration
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Symmetrical length-dependent sensory loss fits dorsal-column and peripheral involvement of B12 deficiency.
Stocking numbness is shared with peripheral neuropathies of other causes.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sensory ataxia / balance failing in the dark or with eyes closed (dorsal-column loss)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Vitamin B12 deficiency with subacute combined degeneration
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Proprioceptive (dorsal-column) failure with visual compensation is characteristic of subacute combined degeneration.
Pure length-dependent neuropathy does not typically produce this degree of proprioceptive/dorsal-column ataxia.
Atrophic glossitis is a well-recognised mucosal feature of B12 (and other haematinic) deficiency.

symptoms.symptom_name: New, worsening confusion and short-term memory loss

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cognitive impairment is part of the B12 deficiency syndrome; also a red flag requiring in-person delirium assessment.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mechanical fall tonight with inability to stand, no head strike

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Vitamin B12 deficiency with subacute combined degeneration
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The fall is the functional consequence of sensory ataxia; also an independent falls-safety red flag.

symptoms.symptom_name: Strict unsupplemented vegan diet for 40+ years

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: B12 is essentially only dietary from animal products; decades of unsupplemented veganism is a definitive dietary risk factor.

symptoms.symptom_name: Lemon-yellow tint — combined pallor and mild jaundice (ineffective erythropoiesis)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pallor with mild jaundice from intramedullary haemolysis/ineffective erythropoiesis is a classic sign; a carer may observe pallor/yellowness but not grade it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absent ankle and knee reflexes bilaterally

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Vitamin B12 deficiency with subacute combined degeneration
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Areflexia reflects peripheral nerve involvement; combined with dorsal-column signs is typical of SACD. Cannot be tested via chat.
Compression usually produces brisk reflexes/upper-motor-neuron signs rather than areflexia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absent vibration and joint-position sense with positive Romberg

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Vitamin B12 deficiency with subacute combined degeneration
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Dorsal-column sensory loss with a positive Romberg is the hallmark of subacute combined degeneration. Requires in-person examination.
A haemic flow murmur reflects significant anaemia; requires auscultation, unavailable in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Macrocytic anaemia with hypersegmented neutrophils on FBC/film

investigation_details.investigation_name: Full blood count and blood film
investigation_details.expected_result: Macrocytic (raised MCV) anaemia with hypersegmented neutrophils and anisopoikilocytosis
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The megaloblastic process is not confirmed and severity of anaemia is unknown.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Macrocytic anaemia with hypersegmented neutrophils is the haematological signature of megaloblastic (B12/folate) deficiency.
Identical film — cannot distinguish B12 from folate deficiency without assay; hence both must be measured.

investigation_details.investigation_name: Serum vitamin B12 (± methylmalonic acid / homocysteine)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The specific deficiency is unconfirmed and B12-versus-folate cannot be separated, risking folate-alone treatment.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A low serum B12 with the clinical picture confirms the deficiency; only obtainable by venepuncture, never via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: No bowel/bladder incontinence and preserved voluntary leg movement (pertinent negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of sphincter disturbance and preserved leg movement makes a hyperacute complete cord syndrome less likely, but does not exclude it — safety-net actively.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Vitamin B12 deficiency with subacute combined degeneration of the cord
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Symmetrical distal sensory loss + sensory ataxia + areflexia + dorsal-column signs together point strongly to SACD rather than an isolated peripheral neuropathy or a compressive lesion.
Dietary risk + glossitis + pallor/jaundice + macrocytic anaemia is the haematinic-deficiency pattern; B12-versus-folate needs assay.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood count and blood film
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms macrocytic/megaloblastic anaemia and gauges severity; first-line and part of the urgent same-day bloods.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum vitamin B12 and serum folate
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Separates B12 from folate deficiency — essential before any replacement so folate is not given alone in a B12-deficient patient with neurological signs.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person neurological examination and falls/mobility assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Documents reflexes, vibration, proprioception, Romberg and power, and assesses fall injury — none obtainable via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Spinal imaging (MRI) if compressive features emerge
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Reserved for evolving or focal cord signs; not routinely first-line here given the symmetrical, areflexic, insidious pattern, but mandated if leg paralysis or sphincter change develops.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Empirical high-dose folate before B12 assay
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Folate replacement before excluding/treating B12 deficiency can precipitate or worsen subacute combined degeneration — an explicit do-not-do.
